channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man in Pennsylvania being questioned over UnitedHealthcare CEO killing: sources
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania is being questioned Monday in connection to the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal challenging Hawaii gun licensing requirements under Second Amendment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court rejected a challenge to Hawaii's gun-licensing law on Monday, though three justices expressed a willingness to hear arguments over the issue later. The majority did not explain their reasoning in a brief order declining to take the case.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police divers and dogs return to Central Park as search for UnitedHeathcare CEO's killer wears on
item.description: Police dogs and divers have returned to New York’s Central Park as a dragnet for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer stretched into a sixth day.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rare cloud formation spotted in Tennessee
item.description: NewsNation affiliate WJHL viewers captured stunning, rare clouds in the Tri-Cities area of Tennessee on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Faith Little, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Boston school admissions policy challenge as Alito, Thomas dissent
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a group of white and Asian parents’ challenge to a temporary admissions policy at Boston’s prestigious “exam schools” over dissents from two of the court’s conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polygamist with 20 'wives' to be sentenced for sex acts with children
item.description: A polygamous religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives ” including 10 underage girls faces decades in prison at his sentencing on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, Fox 5

item.title: Kennedy Center pays tribute to Coppola, the Grateful Dead, Raitt, Sandoval and The Apollo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — “Not Fade Away” closed out the Kennedy Center Honors at this year's ceremony, just as honorees The Grateful Dead had used Buddy Holly’s ode to enduring love to close out hundreds of concerts over the years.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netflix must pay $385K to woman exposed as fertility doctor's secret child: court
item.description: A federal court in Indianapolis has ordered Netflix to pay $385,000 to an Indiana woman it outed as the daughter of Donald Cline, a former fertility doctor who secretly used his own sperm to inseminate patients.
item.dc:creator: Austin Hanson, KTLA 5

item.title: Allen sets NFL mark with 3 TD passes and 3 TD runs, but Stafford's Rams hold off Bills 44-42
item.description: Matthew Stafford and the Los Angeles Rams had to pile up a veritable mountain of points, yards and time-of-possession advantage early on to give themselves even a chance against the Buffalo Bills' ferocious fourth-quarter rally and a historic six-touchdown performance by Josh Allen.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cracker Barrel 'refused service' to students with disabilities, Maryland school says
item.description: Cracker Barrel has issued an apology after a Maryland school district said a group of students with disabilities was denied service at one of the chain's restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Skyler Sales, WGN 9

item.title: Trump names Alina Habba as counselor to president
item.description: “Alina has been a tireless advocate for Justice, a fierce Defender of the Rule of Law, and an invaluable Advisor to my Campaign and Transition Team,” Trump said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Jay-Z accused of sexually assaulting 13-year-old in 2000 with Sean 'Diddy' Combs
item.description: Jay-Z said his only heartbreak is for his family, as having to explain the allegations will be hard on the Carter-Knowles' three children.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A timeline of the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson and search for his killer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The search for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson ’s killer since Thompson was ambushed Wednesday outside a Midtown Manhattan hotel has stretched beyond New York City.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 recipes from the pros to give after-dinner cocktails some holiday flair
item.description: From a festive Old Fashioned to a boozy hot chocolate, these after-dinner drinks might inspire your own menu of holiday cocktails, to be sipped slowly by the fire or washed down with your Christmas pudding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared in Syria in 2012, believed to be alive: Biden
item.description: “We think we can get him back," Biden told reporters at the White House, while acknowledging that “we have no direct evidence” of his status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Forever chemicals tainting food supply, destroying American farmers
item.description: Advocates like Erin Brockovich are sounding the alarm. She says when it comes to forever chemicals, “the storm is here.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, Fox 5

item.title: Giant statue of the Buddha in New Jersey becomes interfaith hub and spiritual home for many
item.description: FRANKLIN TOWNSHIP, N.J. (AP) — Just off a state highway in New Jersey, one of the largest statues of the Buddha in the United States appears unexpectedly in the middle of a backyard.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why work-life balance isn't working for women
item.description: About half of working women reported feeling stressed “a lot of the day," compared to about 4 in 10 men, according to a Gallup report published this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Columbia group's antisemitic newspaper draws outrage from NY lawmaker, as university investigates
item.description: Students at Columbia University launched an anti-Israel paper that was distributed on campus last week, the club announced on social media with images.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These states have the highest — and lowest — median salary: new data
item.description: New data released by a payroll site found median annual salaries from $40,000 to over six figures across the country.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Jokic's career high of 56 points comes during a low point for the Nuggets
item.description: Nikola Jokic scored a career-high 56 points and had little to celebrate afterward. It happened in a loss — to a team that hadn't won since October.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against ‘Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: The nun, 57, is also well-known for her service in Milan's run-down districts. In February she was one of the recipients of the "Golden Panettone", an annual Milanese civic award.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Settlement offers nearly $9M to Louisiana nursing home residents kept in warehouse during hurricane
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Some of the elderly residents of seven Louisiana nursing homes who were sent in 2021 to ride out Hurricane Ida in a crowded, ill-equipped warehouse are being offered shares of a nearly $9 million settlement after they sued.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff's Office for sting operations between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disneyland announces closure date for Red Car Trolley attraction
item.description: Disneyland has announced a closing date for the Red Car Trolley attraction at Disney California Adventure as construction for an expansion of Avengers Campus nears.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Miller High Life releases perfume that smells like a dive bar
item.description: If you've ever wondered how to describe the smell of your small local neighborhood bar, Miller High Life may have just provided that answer for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos show face of 'person of interest' wanted in shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The NYPD released the photos amid a city-wide manhunt for the gunman who shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame reopens its doors to Macron and other world leaders in a rare symbol of unity
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, Fox 5

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, WGN 9

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
